calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
TENTATIVE RULING: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
EXAMINER: AERO FETH

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Continued To Jun-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Freedman for petitioner Myrna Baldemor Mayuga (via Zoom).
Robert White for objector Theodore Liouliakis (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and Status on Mediation.

Counsel reported that the matter had settled in mediation. Mr. Freedman requested that the special administrator's letters be extended to allow time to implement the settlement.

Following discussion, the Court ordered counsel to submit a proposed order extending the special administrator's letters and continued the matter to June 9, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court took the status of mediation off calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Continued To Feb-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Freedman for petitioner Myrna Baldemor Mayuga, who was also present.
Seth Skootsky for objector Theodore Liouliakis (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary.

The Court noted that a will contest had been filed and stated that additional time would be required to allow for proper service. Counsel agreed that a continuance would be appropriate. The Court continued the petition to February 10, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Mr. Freedman stated that an order appointing special administrator had been entered, but the letters expire on January 1, 2026. He requested an extension of special administration to the continued hearing date. The Court agreed and instructed Mr. Freedman to submit a proposed order.

Counsel jointly requested a referral to the Court's pro bono mediation program. The Court approved the request and signed an order referring the case to pro bono mediation in open court. Counsel were instructed to obtain a copy of the order from the Court's website. Mediation was ordered to be completed no later than January 30, 2026. Status on mediation was set for hearing on February 10, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel were directed to notify the assigned Probate Examiner of the status of mediation by February 3, 2026.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
Off Calendar Oct-14-2025 Continued To Oct-17-25 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 202

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-30-2025 Continued To Oct-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Freedman appeared via zoom for and with Myrna Myuga (petitioner).
Seth Scootsky appeared via zoom for Theodore Liouliakis (objector).

Mr. Scootsky from the outset of the hearing said that his client is objecting to this petition, that he filed a declaration September 25, 2025, that he is requesting a continuance, that a will contest needs to be filed in Canada, and that the daughters have a right to contest being disinherited.

Mr. Freedman disagrees with the objection, that Myrna is Danilo's spouse, that Myrna is the sole beneficiary, that he filed A petition for letter of special administration with general powers so that Myrna can be substituted in to handle the decedents pending actions. Also, the Holloway property in San Francisco is the only asset and she holds 50% interest in the property.

The matter is continued to October 21, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Mr. Scootsky to file his written objections by October 14, 2025.


Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Continued To Sep-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
